title: ugetprofile
text: ugetprofile Return a pointer to the name of the current .ini file. Explain this code unsigned char* ugetprofile (void); Copy Parameters None. Return Values A pointer to the name of the current .ini file. Use Microsoft Windows platforms only. In library yrtl . Description ugetprofile returns a pointer to the name of the current .ini file. With this name, you can read and write the .ini file yourself, using either Microsoft Windows API calls such as GetPrivateProfileString and WritePrivateProfileString , or the Uniface 3GL service functions ureadstringsyssetting and uwritestringsyssetting . The current .ini file is usually usys.ini in the USYSADM: directory, unless you use the /ini command line switch to specify another. Note that, even in this case, Uniface still reads the usys.ini file and uses it as a fallback resource when you retrieve data using ureadstringsyssetting . The following code puts the name of the current .ini file into the transcript window: Explain this code #include <h3gl.h> u_printf("Your current ini-file is %s\n", ugetprofile()); Copy Related Links ureadstringsyssetting Read a string from the specified section of a specified ini file. uwritestringsyssetting Copies a string to a specified section of a specified ini file.
